tossups.question: This sculptor’s statue of a shrouded woman resting her cheek on her right hand was inspired by Japanese Buddhist art introduced to him by John La Farge. African-American model Hettie Anderson posed for a winged statue of Victory by this sculptor. Robert Treat Paine created the “cubical pantograph” while working at this sculptor’s Cornish Art Colony. An angel holds a plaque reading “Amor Caritas” in a motif used by this sculptor, who (*) Stanford White commissioned to design a statue of Diana for Madison Square Garden. At Henry Hobson Richardson’s suggestion, this artist created a bronze sculpture inscribed with “he relinquished everything to serve the Republic,” which depicts soldiers with rifles accompanying a colonel on horseback. For 10 points, name this sculptor of the Robert Gould Shaw Memorial.
tossups.question_sanitized: This sculptor's statue of a shrouded woman resting her cheek on her right hand was inspired by Japanese Buddhist art introduced to him by John La Farge. African-American model Hettie Anderson posed for a winged statue of Victory by this sculptor. Robert Treat Paine created the "cubical pantograph" while working at this sculptor's Cornish Art Colony. An angel holds a plaque reading "Amor Caritas" in a motif used by this sculptor, who (*) Stanford White commissioned to design a statue of Diana for Madison Square Garden. At Henry Hobson Richardson's suggestion, this artist created a bronze sculpture inscribed with "he relinquished everything to serve the Republic," which depicts soldiers with rifles accompanying a colonel on horseback. For 10 points, name this sculptor of the Robert Gould Shaw Memorial.

tossups.question: While in Rome, this artist accepted a commission to create a portrait of Eva Rohr as Marguerite from Gounod's Faust. This artist's brother depicted Ceres carrying a sickle and a sheaf of wheat, and Thales holding a thunderbolt, in his group The Progress of Railroading. He created a standalone artwork of his favored recurring motif consisting of an angel with raised wings wearing a belt and a crown of passion flowers. This artist honored the memory of a woman who committed suicide by ingesting potassium cyanide, by depicting her seated against a granite block, eyes closed and wearing a (*) robe. This man's sculpture of a president rising from a chair, about to give a speech, is located in Lincoln Park. This artist's best known sculpture is a bronze relief in which an angel flies over a man on horseback leading a regiment of black soldiers carrying bayonets. For 10 points, name this American sculptor of the Adams Memorial and the Standing Lincoln, best known for his monument to Robert Gould Shaw.
tossups.question_sanitized: While in Rome, this artist accepted a commission to create a portrait of Eva Rohr as Marguerite from Gounod's Faust. This artist's brother depicted Ceres carrying a sickle and a sheaf of wheat, and Thales holding a thunderbolt, in his group The Progress of Railroading. He created a standalone artwork of his favored recurring motif consisting of an angel with raised wings wearing a belt and a crown of passion flowers. This artist honored the memory of a woman who committed suicide by ingesting potassium cyanide, by depicting her seated against a granite block, eyes closed and wearing a (*) robe. This man's sculpture of a president rising from a chair, about to give a speech, is located in Lincoln Park. This artist's best known sculpture is a bronze relief in which an angel flies over a man on horseback leading a regiment of black soldiers carrying bayonets. For 10 points, name this American sculptor of the Adams Memorial and the Standing Lincoln, best known for his monument to Robert Gould Shaw.

tossups.question: Near the end of his life, this artist created a monument for his birthplace, Dublin, honoring Charles Stuart Parnell. He mentored other American artists at the Cornish Art Colony in New Hampshire and designed a $20 Double Eagle gold piece for the US Mint. His Civil War memorials include an equestrian statue of a (*) Union general in Central Park as well as a sculpture of a president standing thoughtfully in front of a chair with an eagle motif. This sculptor of the Sherman Memorial and Standing Lincoln depicted the leader of the all-black 54th Massachusetts Infantry Regiment in a monument on Boston Common. For 10 points, name this American Renaissance sculptor of the Robert Gould Shaw Memorial.
tossups.question_sanitized: Near the end of his life, this artist created a monument for his birthplace, Dublin, honoring Charles Stuart Parnell. He mentored other American artists at the Cornish Art Colony in New Hampshire and designed a $20 Double Eagle gold piece for the US Mint. His Civil War memorials include an equestrian statue of a (*) Union general in Central Park as well as a sculpture of a president standing thoughtfully in front of a chair with an eagle motif. This sculptor of the Sherman Memorial and Standing Lincoln depicted the leader of the all-black 54th Massachusetts Infantry Regiment in a monument on Boston Common. For 10 points, name this American Renaissance sculptor of the Robert Gould Shaw Memorial.

tossups.question: A Kenyon Cox portrait of this artist alludes to his own work by depicting him in profile, employing his non-traditional additive technique to a clay bas-relief. This artist’s design of a torch-bearing nude in strong contrapposto was overturned after winning a commission, engendering this artist’s long-running feud with Charles Barber. Eugenia Anderson modeled for a gilded statue of victory holding a palm frond that this man made, which stands next to an equestrian statue in Manhattan’s Grand Army Plaza. Liberty strides forward with an olive branch on the (*) obverse of an object this man designed. This man founded an influential art colony in Cornish, New Hampshire. This artist of the Standing Lincoln in Lincoln park created a cast bronze sculpture in which soldiers march around the title equestrian as Victory flies overhead. For 10 points, name this beaux-arts sculptor of a namesake Double Eagle coin, as well as memorials to William T. Sherman and Robert Shaw.
tossups.question_sanitized: A Kenyon Cox portrait of this artist alludes to his own work by depicting him in profile, employing his non-traditional additive technique to a clay bas-relief. This artist's design of a torch-bearing nude in strong contrapposto was overturned after winning a commission, engendering this artist's long-running feud with Charles Barber. Eugenia Anderson modeled for a gilded statue of victory holding a palm frond that this man made, which stands next to an equestrian statue in Manhattan's Grand Army Plaza. Liberty strides forward with an olive branch on the (*) obverse of an object this man designed. This man founded an influential art colony in Cornish, New Hampshire. This artist of the Standing Lincoln in Lincoln park created a cast bronze sculpture in which soldiers march around the title equestrian as Victory flies overhead. For 10 points, name this beaux-arts sculptor of a namesake Double Eagle coin, as well as memorials to William T. Sherman and Robert Shaw.

bonuses.leadin: A popular legend states that the hands of a sculpture of this man depict his initials in American Sign Language. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A popular legend states that the hands of a sculpture of this man depict his initials in American Sign Language. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this man who sits in a chair decorated with fasces (“FASS-eez”) in a Daniel Chester French sculpture located in a namesake memorial overlooking a reflecting pool in Washington, DC.

This artist, who had a son named Lincoln, sculpted a bust of Lincoln now in the United States Capitol crypt. After being fired from one of this artist’s projects, Korczak Ziolkowski (“KOR-chock zee-ol-KOFF-skee”) designed the nearby Crazy Horse Memorial.
A series of bronze Lincoln statues subtitled “The Man” was created by this artist, who founded the Cornish Art Colony and depicted Lady Liberty in a double eagle gold coin designed for the US Mint.
parts_sanitized: Name this man who sits in a chair decorated with fasces ("FASS-eez") in a Daniel Chester French sculpture located in a namesake memorial overlooking a reflecting pool in Washington, DC.

This artist, who had a son named Lincoln, sculpted a bust of Lincoln now in the United States Capitol crypt. After being fired from one of this artist's projects, Korczak Ziolkowski ("KOR-chock zee-ol-KOFF-skee") designed the nearby Crazy Horse Memorial.
A series of bronze Lincoln statues subtitled "The Man" was created by this artist, who founded the Cornish Art Colony and depicted Lady Liberty in a double eagle gold coin designed for the US Mint.
answers: Gutzon Borglum [or John Gutzon de la Mothe Borglum] (Borglum’s Mount Rushmore is about 17 miles away from the Crazy Horse Memorial.)
answers_sanitized: Gutzon Borglum [or John Gutzon de la Mothe Borglum] (Borglum's Mount Rushmore is about 17 miles away from the Crazy Horse Memorial.)

bonuses.leadin: Mark Twain referred to a depiction of one of these animals as “the most mournful and moving piece of stone in the world.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name these animals that Bertel Thorvaldsen sculpted as a monument to Swiss Guards massacred during the French Revolution.

This American sculpted two lions for a Civil War memorial for the main staircase of the Boston Public Library. He made a relief honoring Robert Shaw and the African-American 54th Massachusetts Infantry.
British artist Edwin Landseer sculpted four lions flanking the base of this monument in Trafalgar Square in London.

bonuses.leadin: Originally appearing as three figures for the tomb of Edwin Morgan, this motif consists of a winged angel holding a tablet above its head. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this recurrent motif in the work of a certain artist, which takes its name from the words written on the tablet.
The Amor Caritas motif is prominent in the work of this American sculptor, who is perhaps best known for the Robert Gould Shaw memorial in Boston.
Saint-Gaudens is also known for creating numerous instances of these bronze relief portraits, which included depictions of John Singer Sargent, George Washington, and Theodore Roosevelt.
